evals.prompt: I've dropped our product spec and architecture notes for the customer support RAG agent into project-context/. Use phased-project-builder to get this started.
evals.expected_output: Reads all files in project-context/, produces a Context Summary (goal, requirements, tech stack signals, blockers vs assumptions), proposes a phase-by-phase build plan tailored to an agentic RAG system, and explicitly pauses for approval before writing any code or creating .project/ scaffolding.
expectations: Produces a Context Summary before any phase plan or code, including project name, project type, one-sentence core goal, key requirements, and tech stack signals drawn from the context files
Correctly surfaces the hard PII/VPC constraint from product-spec.md (no third-party APIs may process raw customer data outside the VPC) as either a stated requirement or a blocker/assumption
Proposes a phase-by-phase build plan (multiple discrete phases, each with a goal and deliverables) rather than one big undifferentiated plan
Explicitly pauses and asks for the user's approval before writing any code or creating .project/ scaffolding
Does not create .project/ files or write project source code in this run, since approval was never granted

evals.prompt: New chat, continuing the customer support RAG agent project. Use phased-project-builder.
evals.expected_output: Detects the existing .project/ state without being told to resume, reads PROJECT_STATE.md and ISSUES.md, and immediately presents a Resume Brief (session number, last completed phase, next phase, open issues, overall progress) before asking what to do next -- it does not ask the user to explain the project first.
expectations: Reads .project/PROJECT_STATE.md (and ISSUES.md) before responding, rather than asking the user to explain the project first
Immediately presents a Resume Brief as its first substantive response
The resume brief correctly states the last completed phase (Phase 3) and next phase (Phase 4 - Retrieval Pipeline)
The resume brief correctly states overall progress as 3 of 9 phases complete
Surfaces the one open issue (ISSUE-001, PDF chunking losing section headers) in or immediately after the brief
Ends by asking whether to continue with the next phase or review the open issue first, and pauses

evals.prompt: Let's move on to Phase 7 (Tools & Sub-agents) -- start with the order-lookup tool. Use phased-project-builder.
evals.expected_output: While building the order-lookup tool, notices that project-context/product-spec.md and project-context/addendum-notes-2026-07-08.md give contradictory integration details for the Order Management API (different base URL and auth mechanism). Stops, logs an ISSUE-001 entry in .project/ISSUES.md and .json describing the contradiction, and asks the user how to proceed -- rather than silently picking one version and continuing to build.
expectations: Reads project-context/product-spec.md and project-context/addendum-notes-2026-07-08.md before or while building the order-lookup tool
Detects the contradiction between the two files (different base URL and different auth mechanism -- API key vs OAuth2)
Stops rather than silently picking one version of the API details and continuing to build the tool
Logs an issue entry in .project/ISSUES.md (and ISSUES.json) following the ISSUE-N template (severity, description, context, impact, suggested resolution)
Asks the user how to proceed rather than resolving the contradiction unilaterally
